Resolves #13926

Related: #13860, #13914, #13920, #13924

Adds a graph-independent deployment-state bridge: the internal orchestrator can write a bounded, allowlisted snapshot through the existing deployment runtime holder, while public KB and MC MCP surfaces only get read-only snapshot tools. The public tools do not receive Docker socket, shell, exec, restart, daemon-route, or write-actuator authority, and they tolerate stale deployment overlays by returning explicit unconfigured/missing/stale states instead of throwing on absent config leaves.

Evidence: L2 (unit coverage plus real KB/MC toolService dispatch against a wedged-model/unhealthy-memory snapshot fixture) -> L3 required (post-merge deployment smoke against the private overlay and shared snapshot mount). Residual: live deployment smoke [#13926].

## Deltas from ticket

- Added a stale-overlay guard: KB/MC `get_deployment_state_snapshot` falls back through helper defaults when `orchestrator.deploymentStateBridge` is absent.
- Added direct public-tool smoke coverage for both KB and MC reading the same deployment-state snapshot fixture.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Private deployment overlay enables `NEO_DEPLOYMENT_STATE_BRIDGE_ENABLED`, gives orchestrator a writer path, and mounts the same `NEO_DEPLOYMENT_STATE_BRIDGE_SNAPSHOT_PATH` read-only into KB and MC.
- [ ] Private deployment overlay sets `NEO_DEPLOYMENT_STATE_BRIDGE_ALLOWED_SERVICES` to the intended deployment service keys.
- [ ] Live deployment smoke reads KB and MC `get_deployment_state_snapshot` while the model runner is wedged and Memory Core is unhealthy or graph-unavailable, proving KB remains a current-state path when healthy.

## Commits

- `f3b07a7f37` - add deployment-state bridge writer, snapshot store, KB/MC read tools, config leaves, and focused tests.
- `c699e012b2` - cover public KB/MC read dispatch and stale-overlay-safe tool fallback.
